chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حَدِّ الْمَرَضِ الَّذِي يَجُوزُ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُفْطِرَ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 39 – The limit of the illness which is allowed for the man that he does not Fast therein

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/308)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح على الظاهر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/308)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا حَدُّ الْمَرِيضِ إِذَا نَقَهَ فِي الصِّيَامِ قَالَ ذَلِكَ إِلَيْهِ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِنَفْسِهِ إِذَا قَوِيَ فَلْيَصُمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Shuayb, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What is the limit of the patient when he is convalescing during the Fasting?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is up to him. He is more knowing of himself. When he is strong, so let him Fast’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus ibn Shu’ayb from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the limit of the illness that weakens one when fasting. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is up to him to decide; he knows better about his soul and when he is strong he can then fast.’”
